channel.title: Magick – 0=2 : 93/93 : 418 : Love is the law
channel.description: "We place no reliance on virgin or pigeon"

item.title: The Role of the Ars Goetia within Thelema
item.dc:creator: Tobit
item.description: The Ars Goetia, also known as The Lesser Key of Solomon, is a 17th-century grimoire, or book of magick that describes the 72 demons of Hell, which are known as the Goetia. It is believed that the Goetia can be controlled, or at least bargained with, by practitioners of ceremonial magick. The book is divided into two parts: the first part lists the demons, their names, ranks, and sigils; the second part provides instructions on how to summon and control the demons. The Ars Goetia describes Hell as having a complex, hierarchical political system. Each demon has …
item.content:encoded:
The Ars Goetia, also known as The Lesser Key of Solomon, is a 17th-century grimoire, or book of magick that describes the 72 demons of Hell, which are known as the Goetia. It is believed that the Goetia can be controlled, or at least bargained with, by practitioners of ceremonial magick. The book is divided into two parts: the first part lists the demons, their names, ranks, and sigils; the second part provides instructions on how to summon and control the demons.

The Ars Goetia describes Hell as having a complex, hierarchical political system. Each demon has a specific rank and power, and they are organized under the command of powerful entities known as Goetic Kings or Princes. These kings and princes have authority and are considered leaders within the infernal hierarchy.

At the top of the hierarchy is Satan, who is the “Prince of Hell” and the “Great Emperor of the Infernal Regions.” Satan is assisted by his two lieutenants, Beelzebub and Lucifer. Below Satan are the seven princes of Hell, who each govern a specific legion of demons. The seven princes are:

Baal
Agares
Vassago
Samigina
Marbas
Valefor
Amon

The princes of Hell are further subdivided into lesser principalities, dukes, counts, marquises, and presidents. Each of these demons has a specific rank and function in the infernal hierarchy.

The Ars Goetia also describes the relationship between the demons of Hell and the human world. The demons are said to be able to grant favors to humans, such as wealth, power, and knowledge. However, the demons also demand a price for their favors.

In addition to the demons listed above, the Ars Goetia mentions a number of other demons, including Asmodeus, Lilith, and Mephistopheles. These demons are not ranked as princes, but they play significant roles in the infernal hierarchy.

The Ars Goetia is a controversial text, and its authenticity has been questioned by some scholars. However, the book remains an important source of information on demonology.

Here are some additional thoughts on the politics and principalities of Hell described in the Ars Goetia:

The demons of Hell are said to be constantly vying for power and influence. This leads to conflict and violence, as the demons try to gain an advantage over their rivals.

The demons of Hell are said to be very deceptive. They will often promise humans favors that they never intend to deliver. This can lead to humans being tricked and exploited. Donald Trump supporters can empathize.

The politics and principalities of Hell are a complex, evolving landscape. It is a world where power is constantly shifting, and where deception is the only rule.

The role of the Ars Goetia within Thelema, a religious and philosophical system developed by Aleister Crowley, is complex. Thelema embraces the idea of individual spiritual freedom and personal exploration. While Crowley himself was intrigued by and explored various magical systems, including the Goetic practices in his early years, he later distanced himself from specific grimoires including the Ars Goetia.

Crowley believed that the true practice of Thelema was centered on individuals discovering and expressing their own unique spiritual path and will, rather than strictly adhering to prescribed systems from grimoires. He argued that the Goetia represented an outdated, Christianized perspective on demonic entities and was not aligned with the radical individualism of Thelema.

However, some Thelemites, particularly those who engage in ceremonial magic, may still find value in the Ars Goetia as it provides a framework for evoking and working with various spirits. Ultimately, its role within Thelema depends on the individual practitioner’s personal beliefs and practices.

item.title: Thelema: Eastern and Western Individualism
item.dc:creator: Tobit
item.description: Thelema, as a philosophical and religious system developed by Aleister Crowley in the early 20th century, presents an interesting intersection between Eastern philosophy and the Western enforcement of morality and convention. Thelema draws upon various spiritual traditions, including elements of Western occultism, mysticism, and ancient Egyptian mythology, while also incorporating Eastern philosophical concepts such as the idea of True Will and the pursuit of self-realization. One of the central tenets of Thelema is the concept of True Will, which can be understood as an individual’s unique and authentic purpose in life. Thelemites believe that discovering and aligning …
item.content:encoded:
Thelema, as a philosophical and religious system developed by Aleister Crowley in the early 20th century, presents an interesting intersection between Eastern philosophy and the Western enforcement of morality and convention. Thelema draws upon various spiritual traditions, including elements of Western occultism, mysticism, and ancient Egyptian mythology, while also incorporating Eastern philosophical concepts such as the idea of True Will and the pursuit of self-realization.

One of the central tenets of Thelema is the concept of True Will, which can be understood as an individual’s unique and authentic purpose in life. Thelemites believe that discovering and aligning with one’s True Will is essential for personal growth and fulfillment. This concept shares similarities with Eastern philosophies that emphasize the importance of self-discovery, such as Hinduism’s concept of Dharma or Buddhism’s teachings on finding one’s own path to liberation.

However, Thelema also challenges conventional Western notions of morality and societal norms. It promotes the idea of individual sovereignty and encourages individuals to follow their own desires and inclinations, as long as they do not infringe upon the rights of others. This can be seen as a departure from traditional Western religions that often enforce moral codes and dogmas.

In the context of Eastern philosophy, which often encourages personal transformation and spiritual exploration, Thelema aligns with the idea of individual empowerment and the pursuit of one’s authentic path. However, Thelema’s rejection of conventional morality and its emphasis on individual freedom may be seen as more in line with certain Western countercultural movements that challenged societal norms and advocated for personal liberation.

It is important to note that Thelema, like any belief system, is subject to interpretation and varies among practitioners. While some individuals may emphasize the individualistic and rebellious aspects of Thelema, others may focus more on its spiritual and transformative elements, finding connections with Eastern philosophies that promote self-realization and inner harmony.

In summary, Thelema presents an intriguing blend of Eastern and Western influences. It shares similarities with Eastern philosophies in its emphasis on self-discovery and the pursuit of one’s unique purpose. However, Thelema also challenges Western enforcement of morality and convention by promoting individual sovereignty and freedom. Ultimately, how Thelema fits between the paradigm of Eastern philosophy and Western enforcement of morality and convention can vary depending on the interpretation and emphasis of its practitioners.

item.title: Eastern Philosophies and Western Religions: Exploring Contrasts and Lessons for Humanity
item.dc:creator: Tobit
item.description: Throughout history, humanity has sought answers to profound questions about existence, purpose, and the nature of reality. This quest has led to the development of diverse belief systems and philosophical traditions across the globe. In this post, we will compare the history of Eastern philosophies, their evolutionary development, and the establishment of schools and temples, with the Western approach to religion, characterized by politically motivated churches, witch covens, and the Inquisition. By examining these differences, we can glean valuable insights and lessons for humanity’s collective growth and understanding. Eastern Philosophies: Evolutionary Development and Spiritual Exploration Eastern philosophies …
item.content:encoded:
Throughout history, humanity has sought answers to profound questions about existence, purpose, and the nature of reality. This quest has led to the development of diverse belief systems and philosophical traditions across the globe. In this post, we will compare the history of Eastern philosophies, their evolutionary development, and the establishment of schools and temples, with the Western approach to religion, characterized by politically motivated churches, witch covens, and the Inquisition. By examining these differences, we can glean valuable insights and lessons for humanity’s collective growth and understanding.

Eastern Philosophies: Evolutionary Development and Spiritual Exploration

Eastern philosophies encompass a rich tapestry of thought, including Hinduism, Buddhism, Taoism, and Confucianism, among others. These philosophies originated in ancient times and have evolved through centuries of contemplation and introspection.

One remarkable aspect of Eastern philosophies is their emphasis on spiritual exploration and self-realization. Rather than relying on external authorities or dogmas, these traditions encourage individuals to seek inner wisdom through practices such as meditation, mindfulness, and self-discipline. The schools and temples that emerged from these philosophies became centers of learning and spiritual guidance, fostering a harmonious blend of intellectual pursuit and personal growth.

Western Religions: Politics, Persecution, and the Dark Shadows

In contrast to Eastern philosophies, the history of Western religions, particularly Christianity, has been marred by episodes of political manipulation, persecution, and intolerance. The rise of politically motivated churches, such as the Roman Catholic Church during the Middle Ages, led to the centralization of power, often intertwined with secular authority. This entanglement of religion and politics resulted in a hierarchical structure that sometimes placed the interests of the few above the spiritual well-being of the many.

One particularly dark chapter in Western religious history is the Inquisition. During the Middle Ages and Renaissance, the Inquisition was used as a means to enforce religious orthodoxy and suppress dissenting voices. Countless individuals were persecuted, tortured, and executed, accused of heresy or witchcraft. Witch covens, popularly associated with pagan traditions and nature-based religions, faced similar persecution, with accusations of devil worship and supernatural powers leading to the suffering and death of many innocent people.

Lessons for Humanity: Embracing Tolerance, Self-Exploration, and Harmony

Comparing the histories of Eastern philosophies and Western religions reveals important lessons for humanity. It is crucial to emphasize tolerance, respect, and openness when engaging with differing beliefs and ideas. The emphasis on personal growth and self-exploration in Eastern philosophies offers a path for individuals to develop a deeper understanding of themselves and their place in the world.

Additionally, the examples of politically motivated churches, witch hunts, and the Inquisition serve as cautionary tales about the dangers of power and dogmatism. Humanity must strive to separate spiritual exploration from the manipulation of politics and power. The freedom to express one’s beliefs and engage in dialogue fosters an environment of intellectual growth, mutual understanding, and compassion.

Furthermore, embracing the diversity of spiritual practices can enrich our collective understanding of the human experience. By studying and appreciating various philosophies and religions, we can discover commonalities, explore different perspectives, and build bridges of understanding.

Conclusions:

The comparison of Eastern philosophies and Western religions highlights the evolutionary development and contrasting approaches to spirituality. While Eastern philosophies promote self-exploration and personal growth, Western religions have faced challenges arising from political motivations and persecution.

Humanity can learn valuable lessons from this comparison by embracing tolerance, openness, and self-exploration. We must strive to separate religion from politics, nurturing an environment where diverse beliefs can coexist harmoniously. By fostering such an environment, we can encourage intellectual growth, mutual respect, and compassion among individuals of different faiths and philosophies.

In our modern world, it is essential to recognize the inherent value of diversity in belief systems. Instead of viewing differences as threats or challenges to one’s own faith, we should embrace them as opportunities for personal growth and collective enlightenment. Just as Eastern philosophies have thrived through their acceptance of multiple paths to spiritual realization, we can also appreciate the richness and depth that different religious traditions bring to the tapestry of human existence.

Moreover, the history of politically motivated churches, witch hunts, and the Inquisition serves as a reminder of the dangers of religious extremism and the abuse of power. It compels us to question the influence of political agendas within religious institutions and to safeguard against the suppression of individual freedoms and the persecution of those with differing beliefs.

By promoting dialogue, education, and interfaith understanding, we can break down the barriers that separate us and foster a sense of unity and shared purpose. Humanity can draw inspiration from the wisdom and teachings of Eastern philosophies, which encourage introspection, compassion, and the pursuit of inner harmony.

The comparison between Eastern philosophies and Western religions offers valuable insights and lessons for humanity. By embracing tolerance, self-exploration, and harmonious coexistence, we can create a world where diverse beliefs are celebrated and where spiritual growth is nurtured. Let us strive to learn from history and work towards a future where respect, compassion, and understanding are the guiding principles that unite us in our quest for meaning and purpose.

item.title: The Dynamic Duo: John Dee, Edward Kelly, and Their Profound Influence on Occultism
item.dc:creator: Tobit
item.description: The realm of occultism has always enticed and intrigued seekers of hidden knowledge throughout history. Within this realm, the partnership between John Dee and Edward Kelly stands as a remarkable chapter. Their unique collaboration, the church’s view on their work, their influence on Aleister Crowley, and their impact on chaos magick and modern occultism overall deserve closer examination. 1. The Profound Partnership: In the late 16th century, John Dee, an esteemed English mathematician, philosopher, and astrologer, met Edward Kelly, an alchemist and medium from Bohemia (now the Czech Republic). Their partnership began in 1582 when Kelly claimed …
item.content:encoded:
The realm of occultism has always enticed and intrigued seekers of hidden knowledge throughout history. Within this realm, the partnership between John Dee and Edward Kelly stands as a remarkable chapter. Their unique collaboration, the church’s view on their work, their influence on Aleister Crowley, and their impact on chaos magick and modern occultism overall deserve closer examination.

1. The Profound Partnership:


In the late 16th century, John Dee, an esteemed English mathematician, philosopher, and astrologer, met Edward Kelly, an alchemist and medium from Bohemia (now the Czech Republic). Their partnership began in 1582 when Kelly claimed to be able to communicate with angels. Dee saw an opportunity to unlock divine knowledge and supported Kelly as his seer.

2. The Church’s Perception:


During this period, the church held considerable power and influence. Dee and Kelly’s work often drew suspicion and scrutiny. They were accused of engaging in forbidden practices, particularly by Catholic authorities. The church feared their activities could challenge traditional religious structures and doctrines.

3. Aleister Crowley’s Influence:


Dee and Kelly’s work, particularly their attempt to communicate with angelic beings through a crystal or “scrying” mirror, deeply fascinated Aleister Crowley. Crowley, widely regarded as the influential figure in 20th-century occultism and the founder of Thelema, adopted many concepts from Dee’s and Kelly’s practices. Crowley saw himself as the successor of Dee and inherited their exploration of the spiritual world.

4. Impact on Chaos Magick:


Chaos magick, a modern occult practice aiming to tap into personal power and change reality, drew inspiration from the works of Dee and Kelly. The chaos magick movement saw ritualistic structures as mere tools for the manipulation of reality, and thus, drew on the elemental power Dee and Kelly possessed. The fluidity and innovation inherent in chaos magick stemmed from the spirit of experimentation pioneered by these two historical figures.

5. Influence on Modern Occultism:


John Dee and Edward Kelly fundamentally shaped modern occultism in numerous ways. Their practices and beliefs opened the door to alternative modes of spirituality and the exploration of non-traditional avenues. Their approach to ritual, scrying, and spirit communication challenged established religious practices and dogmas, paving the way for an increasingly pluralistic and diverse occult landscape.

The partnership between John Dee and Edward Kelly not only pioneered new spiritual practices but also challenged the religious establishment of their time. Their controversial endeavors and unorthodox paths deeply influenced the likes of Aleister Crowley, the rise of chaos magick, and modern occultism as a whole. In today’s world, their legacy lives on as a symbol of the unyielding human pursuit of esoteric wisdom and the enduring impact of their contribution to the ever-evolving occult tradition.

item.title: Unveiling the Mysteries of Enochian Magic: History, Application, and Lasting Influence
item.dc:creator: Tobit
item.description: Enochian Magic, a complex and enigmatic system of occult practice, has captivated the minds of magicians, scholars, and occult enthusiasts for centuries. Rooted in the supposed communication with angels and the exploration of higher realms, Enochian Magic is a unique and influential aspect of Western esotericism. In this essay, we will explore the history, application, practice, and philosophy of Enochian Magic, as well as its profound influence on future occult schools of thought. 1. History of Enochian Magic: Enochian Magic traces its origins to the late 16th century, attributed to the English occultists Dr. John Dee, a …
item.content:encoded:
Enochian Magic, a complex and enigmatic system of occult practice, has captivated the minds of magicians, scholars, and occult enthusiasts for centuries. Rooted in the supposed communication with angels and the exploration of higher realms, Enochian Magic is a unique and influential aspect of Western esotericism. In this essay, we will explore the history, application, practice, and philosophy of Enochian Magic, as well as its profound influence on future occult schools of thought.

1. History of Enochian Magic:


Enochian Magic traces its origins to the late 16th century, attributed to the English occultists Dr. John Dee, a mathematician, astronomer, and advisor to Queen Elizabeth I, and his scryer Edward Kelley. They claimed to have received a complex system of angelic communication and magical practices from celestial entities, including the language of angels called Enochian.

2. Application and Practice of Enochian Magic:


a. Angelic Communication: At the core of Enochian Magic is the belief that practitioners can communicate with higher intelligences, specifically angelic beings. Dee and Kelley believed that the angelic language, Enochian, provided access to profound spiritual insights, divinatory knowledge, and practical magical operations.


b. The Heptarchia Mystica: One significant aspect of Enochian Magic is the Heptarchia Mystica, a system of planetary and angelic invocations. It involves invoking and working with the seven planetary angelic governors and their associated forces, harnessing their powers for various purposes.


c. Elemental Tablets and Watchtower System: Enochian Magic incorporates a complex system of elemental tablets and watchtowers, which represent different regions of the spiritual and physical realms. Practitioners use these tools to establish contact with various entities, invoke energies, and perform rituals.

3. Philosophy of Enochian Magic:


a. Cosmology: Enochian Magic encompasses a unique cosmology that involves hierarchical spiritual realms, angelic hierarchies, and the interplay between different planes of existence. It emphasizes the exploration and harmonization of these realms for spiritual growth and magical power.


b. Divine Language and Harmonics: Enochian Magic holds that the angelic language, Enochian, possesses inherent vibrational power and harmony. The use of this language and its accompanying rituals is believed to resonate with divine frequencies and facilitate contact with angelic forces.


c. Ascension and Spiritual Evolution: Enochian Magic promotes the idea of spiritual evolution and ascension through the exploration of higher realms. Practitioners seek to elevate their consciousness, refine their spiritual faculties, and attain a state of unity with divine forces.

4. Influence on Future Occult Schools of Thought:


Enochian Magic has left a profound impact on various occult schools of thought, inspiring and influencing practitioners and scholars alike. Some notable areas of influence include:


a. The Hermetic Order of the Golden Dawn: Enochian Magic played a significant role in the rituals and teachings of the Hermetic Order of the Golden Dawn. It contributed to their understanding of angelic forces, symbolism, and ritual practices.


b. Thelema and Aleister Crowley: Thelema, a system of spiritual and magical practice developed by Aleister Crowley, drew upon Enochian Magic as an integral part of its ceremonial practices. Enochian elements can be found in Crowley’s rituals and his exploration of angelic communication.


c. Modern Magick and Eclectic Traditions: Enochian Magic continues to influence modern magickal systems and eclectic occult traditions. Many contemporary practitioners incorporate Enochian techniques, rituals, and symbology into their magical practices. The flexibility and adaptability of Enochian Magic make it a valuable resource for those seeking to incorporate angelic communication and higher realms into their personal spiritual paths.

Additionally, the study of Enochian Magic has had a lasting impact on academic and scholarly circles. Its complex system of angelic communication, symbolism, and cosmology has sparked extensive research and exploration. Scholars continue to analyze and interpret the texts and manuscripts associated with Enochian Magic, shedding light on its historical context and its influence on occultism.

Moreover, Enochian Magic’s emphasis on angelic communication and the exploration of higher realms resonates with broader themes found in various mystical and spiritual traditions. The concept of connecting with higher intelligences, seeking spiritual insights, and harnessing divine forces can be seen in different cultural and religious contexts throughout history.

Enochian Magic stands as a remarkable and enduring aspect of Western esotericism. Its history, application, practice, and philosophy provide a rich tapestry of knowledge and insight into the realms of angelic communication and higher spiritual realms. Its influence on future occult schools of thought, such as the Golden Dawn, Thelema, and modern eclectic traditions, showcases the lasting impact and adaptability of this magical system. As practitioners and scholars continue to explore the depths of Enochian Magic, its mysteries and wisdom continue to unfold, inviting individuals to embark on their own personal journeys of spiritual discovery and transformation.

item.title: The Power of Parody Religions: Finding Kinship and Healing Through Satire
item.dc:creator: Tobit
item.description: In a world dominated by mainstream religions, there exists a fascinating realm of parody religions that challenge the conventional norms and offer a unique perspective on spirituality. These playful and satirical movements, such as the Church of the SubGenius, the Church of the Flying Spaghetti Monster (Pastafarianism), Discordianism, and the Principia Discordia, may seem unconventional or even nonsensical at first glance. However, they serve a profound purpose by providing solace, community, and a sense of belonging to those who find themselves at odds with traditional religious institutions. In this blog post, we will explore how these so-called …
item.content:encoded:
In a world dominated by mainstream religions, there exists a fascinating realm of parody religions that challenge the conventional norms and offer a unique perspective on spirituality. These playful and satirical movements, such as the Church of the SubGenius, the Church of the Flying Spaghetti Monster (Pastafarianism), Discordianism, and the Principia Discordia, may seem unconventional or even nonsensical at first glance. However, they serve a profound purpose by providing solace, community, and a sense of belonging to those who find themselves at odds with traditional religious institutions. In this blog post, we will explore how these so-called “fake” religions can be surprisingly healthy, offering a haven for individuals who may not fit in socially and fostering a sense of kinship with others.

1. The Church of the SubGenius: Embracing Absurdity as a Path to Liberation

The Church of the SubGenius, founded in the 1980s, thrives on the idea of embracing absurdity. By blending elements of conspiracy theories, science fiction, and humor, it presents itself as a parody of religious dogma. While it may appear chaotic and nonsensical on the surface, SubGenius philosophy encourages followers to question authority, reject societal norms, and find personal liberation. In a society that often expects conformity, the SubGenius movement offers an outlet for those who feel alienated or disillusioned by established structures.

Within the framework of the Church of the SubGenius, the concepts of the Xists, JR “Bob” Dobbs, and Slack play significant roles. Let’s explore each of these elements:

1. The Xists: The Xists are extraterrestrial beings depicted in the teachings of the Church of the SubGenius. According to the church’s mythology, the Xists are a race of alien overlords who plan to enslave humanity. They represent a satirical critique of conspiracy theories, parodies of apocalyptic prophecies, and the fear of external control.

The Church of the SubGenius uses the concept of the Xists to explore themes of individual sovereignty, skepticism towards authority, and the subversion of mainstream beliefs. The Xists serve as a fictional construct that encourages followers to question societal norms and reject oppressive systems.

2. JR “Bob” Dobbs: JR “Bob” Dobbs is the iconic figurehead and self-proclaimed prophet of the Church of the SubGenius. Often depicted as a mysterious salesman with a pipe and a wide-brimmed hat, Dobbs embodies the spirit of the trickster, a sly character who challenges established norms and promotes individual freedom.

Dobbs represents a satirical take on religious leaders and charismatic figures. The Church of the SubGenius uses the persona of Dobbs to encourage followers to find their own path to enlightenment, embrace absurdity, and seek personal liberation. The character of JR “Bob” Dobbs serves as a symbol of the pursuit of “Slack.”

3. Slack: Slack is a central concept in the teachings of the Church of the SubGenius. It is often described as a state of being characterized by personal fulfillment, satisfaction, and freedom from oppressive forces. Slack represents an individual’s ability to break free from societal expectations, find their true purpose, and live a life of enjoyment and fulfillment.

The Church of the SubGenius sees Slack as a valuable resource that mainstream society seeks to suppress and control. Through humor and satire, the church encourages its followers to seek and embrace Slack by rejecting conformity, questioning authority, and embracing their unique quirks and desires.

Overall, the Church of the SubGenius uses the concepts of the Xists, JR “Bob” Dobbs, and Slack to challenge societal norms, question authority, and promote personal liberation. These elements serve as satirical devices that encourage followers to find their own path, reject oppressive systems, and embrace the absurdity of existence.

2. Pastafarianism: A Delicious Take on Satire and Freedom of Expression

Pastafarianism, symbolized by the Flying Spaghetti Monster, emerged as a response to the teaching of intelligent design in schools. It uses satire to criticize the encroachment of religious beliefs into public education. The Church of the Flying Spaghetti Monster may seem whimsical, with its pirate regalia and reverence for pasta, but beneath the humorous facade lies a powerful message: the promotion of scientific literacy, secularism, and the right to express one’s beliefs in unconventional ways. Pastafarianism has been successful in challenging the privileging of certain religious beliefs over others and highlighting the importance of separation between church and state.

3. Discordianism: Embracing Chaos and Illuminating the Absurdities of Life

Discordianism, inspired by the Principia Discordia, is a parody religion that revels in chaos and embraces absurdity. With a playful approach to spirituality, Discordians reject dogma and instead celebrate randomness, humor, and self-expression. The Principia Discordia outlines the “5 Suggestions” which encourage individuals to find humor in life, question authority, and celebrate the absurdities that permeate our existence. By challenging the seriousness and rigidity often associated with traditional religions, Discordianism fosters an environment of camaraderie and self-discovery.

According to the Principia Discordia, the story of how the Golden Apple fell is a central myth within Discordianism. Here is a summary of the tale as described in the Principia Discordia:

In the beginning, there was chaos, represented by the goddess Eris, the Greek goddess of strife and discord. Eris, known as Discordia in Discordianism, was not invited to the wedding of Peleus and Thetis, a mortal and a sea nymph. Feeling slighted, Eris decided to disrupt the festivities by throwing a golden apple into the midst of the goddesses attending the wedding.

The apple was inscribed with the words “To the Fairest,” leading to a dispute among the goddesses as each claimed the title for herself. The three primary contenders for the apple were Hera, Athena, and Aphrodite. Unable to resolve the argument, they sought the judgment of Paris, a mortal prince.

In return for his decision, each goddess offered Paris a bribe. Hera promised him power and riches, Athena offered him wisdom and victory in battle, and Aphrodite tempted him with the love of the most beautiful woman in the world, Helen of Troy. Paris, swayed by Aphrodite’s offer, awarded her the golden apple.

This act sparked the Trojan War, a legendary conflict in Greek mythology. The story of the Golden Apple falling symbolizes the chaos and discord that can arise from small acts or decisions, setting in motion significant consequences.

In Discordianism, the story of the Golden Apple falling is often interpreted metaphorically. It represents the disruptive power of discord and the recognition that chaos and disorder are integral parts of existence. Embracing the chaos and discord, Discordians find liberation and meaning in the absurdity of life.

The “Discordian Bible”, Principia Discordia, has in place of ten commandments, five suggestions, known collectively as the Pentabarf. The Pentabarf was, according to Discordian legend, discovered by the hermit Apostle Zarathud in the Fifth Year of The Caterpillar. He found them carved in gilded stone, while building a sun deck for his cave, but their import was lost for they were written in a mysterious cypher. However, after 10 weeks & 11 hours of intensive scrutiny he discerned that the message could be read by standing on his head and viewing it upside down. It read:

____________________________________________________
KNOW YE THIS O MAN OF FAITH!

I – There is no Goddess but Goddess and She is Your Goddess. There is no Erisian Movement but The Erisian Movement and it is The Erisian Movement. And every Golden Apple Corps is the beloved home of a Golden Worm.

II – A Discordian Shall Always use the Official Discordian Document Numbering System.

III – A Discordian is Required during his early Illumination to Go Off Alone & Partake Joyously of a Hot Dog on a Friday; this Devotive Ceremony to Remonstrate against the popular Paganisms of the Day: of Catholic Christendom (no meat on Friday), of Judaism (no meat of Pork), of Hindic Peoples (no meat of Beef), of Buddhists (no meat of animal), and of Discordians (no Hot Dog Buns).

IV – A Discordian shall Partake of No Hot Dog Buns, for Such was the Solace of Our Goddess when She was Confronted with The Original Snub.

V – A Discordian is Prohibited of Believing what he reads.

IT IS SO WRITTEN! SO BE IT. HAIL DISCORDIA! PROSECUTORS WILL BE TRANSGRESSICUTED.

__________________________________________________________________________________________

The metaphor in this text, as in the book, is as clear as it is beautiful.

I – This commandment asserts the primacy of the individual’s personal connection with the divine. It states that there is no specific goddess or deity to worship, but rather that each individual’s own understanding of the divine is valid and should be honored. This promotes a sense of personal sovereignty and encourages followers to define their own spiritual beliefs.

II – This commandment humorously states that Discordians should use the Official Discordian Document Numbering System. It reflects the playful and irreverent nature of Discordianism, emphasizing the importance of embracing absurdity and disregarding conventional numbering systems.

III – This commandment instructs Discordians, during their early spiritual journey or “Illumination,” to have a devotional ceremony of joyously partaking in a hot dog on a Friday. The ceremony is meant to contrast with the dietary restrictions or taboos found in various religions, highlighting the satirical nature of Discordianism and its rejection of rigid belief systems.

IV – This commandment extends from the previous one, stating that Discordians should not partake of hot dog buns. The reason given is a reference to the “Original Snub,” a humorous event involving the goddess Eris (Discordia) and a hot dog bun. It further highlights the nonsensical and satirical nature of Discordian practices.

V – This commandment prohibits Discordians from blindly believing everything they read. It promotes a skeptical and critical approach to information, encouraging followers to question, analyze, and discern the truth for themselves. It reflects the emphasis on individual thinking and personal responsibility within Discordianism.

These suggestions serve as a playful and satirical framework for navigating life, embracing chaos, and challenging conventional thinking, while playfully emphasizes the ever-changing and contradictory nature of existence. Discordians are encouraged to embrace the fluidity of their beliefs and opinions, recognizing that consistency can sometimes stifle growth and creativity. By embracing contradiction, Discordians invite a more flexible and open-minded approach to life, allowing for the exploration of diverse perspectives and ideas. Discordianism embraces absurdity and nonconformity, and symbolizes the celebration of individuality, encouraging followers to express themselves freely and authentically.

4. The Healing Power of Parody Religions: Finding Kinship and Belonging

a) A Safe Space for Nonconformists: Parody religions provide a refuge for individuals who feel alienated or marginalized by mainstream religious institutions. They offer a sense of community, acceptance, and belonging to those who may not fit neatly into societal or religious molds. By embracing humor and irreverence, these movements create spaces where people can freely express themselves without fear of judgment or exclusion.

b) Satire as Catharsis: Parody religions act as a cathartic release for individuals who have been hurt or disillusioned by religious dogma or authoritarian structures. Through satire and absurdity, followers can find solace and healing, reclaiming their autonomy and finding humor in the very institutions that may have caused them pain or confusion.

c) Fostering Creativity and Critical Thinking: Parody religions encourage followers to think critically, question established beliefs, and challenge the status quo. By satirizing religious and societal norms, these movements inspire creative and independent thinking, allowing individuals to explore alternative perspectives and expand their intellectual horizons. The freedom to question and challenge authority within parody religions promotes a culture of intellectual curiosity and fosters personal growth.

d) Building Supportive Communities: Parody religions create tight-knit communities where individuals can find support, empathy, and understanding. By gathering like-minded people who share a similar sense of humor and skepticism, these movements form social networks that provide emotional support and camaraderie. In a world where many struggle to find acceptance, parody religions offer a space where people can form meaningful connections and forge lifelong friendships.

e) Challenging Religious Privilege and Promoting Tolerance: Parody religions serve as a powerful tool for challenging religious privilege and promoting religious tolerance. By parodying established faiths and presenting themselves as equally valid, they highlight the arbitrary nature of religious beliefs. This satirical approach prompts important conversations about the boundaries of religious freedom, the separation of church and state, and the need for respect and inclusively towards diverse belief systems.

In closing, these parody religions may initially seem frivolous or absurd, but they offer much more than meets the eye. Thy provide solace, community, and a sense of belonging to individuals who may not fit into traditional religious structures or feel disillusioned by mainstream beliefs. By embracing humor, satire, and irreverence, these movements offer an alternative path for spiritual exploration, personal liberation, and critical thinking. Through their inclusive and supportive communities, parody religions provide a haven for nonconformists, fostering kinship and acceptance. In a world that often demands conformity, these movements remind us of the importance of individuality, creative expression, and the healing power of laughter. They are a humorous call to embrace all beliefs and perspectives, no matter how contradictory or absurd they may seem. They highlight the inherent subjectivity and relativity of truth, emphasizing that individual interpretations and experiences shape our understanding of reality. By embracing a multitude of beliefs, they promote tolerance and inclusivity, encouraging others to respect diverse viewpoints.

The Suggestions of Discordianism, like the philosophy behind many parody religions, are meant to be taken with a sense of humor and irony. They serve as a reminder to challenge rigid thinking, embrace chaos, and find joy in the absurdities of life. Through these playful suggestions, we can create a framework that celebrates individuality, critical thinking, and the liberation of the mind. These core principles embrace absurdity, skepticism, and the challenging of established beliefs, using humor and satire as tools for personal liberation and subversion of societal norms. And the Goddess knows, we need more subversion of the norm.

item.title: Is Magick “Real”?
item.dc:creator: Tobit
item.description: The concept of magic, or magick, is subjective and can be interpreted in various ways depending on one’s beliefs, cultural background, and personal experiences. It is a topic that has been debated and explored throughout human history. In the context of occult and esoteric traditions, magick is often understood as the practice of using ritual, intention, and focused energy to bring about desired outcomes or to connect with spiritual forces. This practice can involve various techniques such as visualization, meditation, energy manipulation, and working with symbols or correspondences. While some individuals and spiritual traditions firmly believe in …
item.content:encoded:
The concept of magic, or magick, is subjective and can be interpreted in various ways depending on one’s beliefs, cultural background, and personal experiences. It is a topic that has been debated and explored throughout human history.

In the context of occult and esoteric traditions, magick is often understood as the practice of using ritual, intention, and focused energy to bring about desired outcomes or to connect with spiritual forces. This practice can involve various techniques such as visualization, meditation, energy manipulation, and working with symbols or correspondences.

While some individuals and spiritual traditions firmly believe in the reality and effectiveness of magick, it is important to note that scientific evidence for the existence of supernatural or metaphysical phenomena associated with magick is limited. The scientific method relies on empirical observation, experimentation, and reproducibility, which may not easily align with the subjective and personal nature of magickal experiences.

It is also important to distinguish between magick and stage magic or illusions performed by entertainers. Stage magic involves sleight of hand, misdirection, and theatrical tricks intended for entertainment purposes, while magick in an occult or spiritual context is concerned with metaphysical and transformative experiences.

Within the context of Thelema, the belief in the reality of magick is a fundamental aspect of the philosophy and practices associated with this system. Aleister Crowley, the influential occultist and founder of Thelema, emphasized the importance of magick as a transformative tool for spiritual growth and self-realization.

In Thelema, magick is seen as a means to discover and align with one’s true will, which is considered to be the individual’s unique purpose and destiny. It is believed that through the practice of magick, individuals can tap into their inner power, connect with higher spiritual forces, and manifest their intentions in accordance with their true will.

Crowley developed a comprehensive system of magickal practices within the framework of Thelema, including rituals, ceremonies, divination, meditation, and energy work. These practices aim to facilitate the exploration of consciousness, the development of psychic abilities, and the transformation of one’s self.

Thelema views magick not only as a means of personal transformation but also as a way to affect change in the external world. It embraces the idea that individuals can influence events, circumstances, and even the collective consciousness through their magickal intentions and actions.

It is important to note that the efficacy and reality of magick within Thelema, like in any other spiritual or occult tradition, are subjective and based on personal experience and belief. While some practitioners of Thelema report profound and transformative experiences through their magickal practices, others may approach it from a symbolic or psychological perspective.

Ultimately, whether magick is considered real within the context of Thelema is a personal belief and can vary among individuals who engage with the philosophy and practices of Thelema. It is up to each practitioner to explore, experiment, and interpret their experiences to determine the nature and reality of magick within their own practice.

item.title: Aleister Crowley’s Insights Into Guardian Angels in Thelema: Unveiling the Sacred Path
item.dc:creator: Tobit
item.description: Aleister Crowley, the influential occultist and founder of Thelema, approached the concept of guardian angels with his distinctive perspective. In Thelema, a spiritual and philosophical system, the understanding of guardian angels differs from traditional notions. This blog post will delve into Crowley’s thoughts on guardian angels within the context of Thelema and explore the practices and principles associated with them. 1. Guardian Angels in Thelema: In Thelema, guardian angels are referred to as Holy Guardian Angels (HGA). They play a significant role in the spiritual journey, serving as personal guides and mentors providing guidance, insight, and protection. …
item.content:encoded:
Aleister Crowley, the influential occultist and founder of Thelema, approached the concept of guardian angels with his distinctive perspective. In Thelema, a spiritual and philosophical system, the understanding of guardian angels differs from traditional notions. This blog post will delve into Crowley’s thoughts on guardian angels within the context of Thelema and explore the practices and principles associated with them.

1. Guardian Angels in Thelema:


In Thelema, guardian angels are referred to as Holy Guardian Angels (HGA). They play a significant role in the spiritual journey, serving as personal guides and mentors providing guidance, insight, and protection. The HGA is considered an essential and sacred connection to the divine, reinforcing the core principle of Thelema – the pursuit of one’s True Will.

2. The Knowledge and Conversation of the Holy Guardian Angel:


One of the fundamental practices in Thelema is the Knowledge and Conversation of the Holy Guardian Angel (K&CHGA). Crowley considered this ritual to be of utmost importance for spiritual advancement and self-discovery. The ritual aims to establish a direct and personal relationship with one’s HGA, allowing individuals to attain true knowledge of their purpose and divine path.

3. Crowley’s Personal Experience:


Aleister Crowley underwent transformative spiritual experiences himself when it came to the K&CHGA ritual. He believed that contact with the HGA enabled a profound expansion of consciousness, leading to a deeper understanding of one’s innermost desires, talents, and Limitless potential. Crowley considered this transformative process to be central to the practice of Thelema.

4. Thelema’s Approach to Guardian Angels:


In Thelema, the relationship with the HGA is intensely personal and unique to each individual. Unlike traditional angelic beliefs that connote external beings, Thelema views the HGA as a reflection of one’s higher self or divine aspect. The HGA represents the embodiment of one’s True Will and acts as a guiding force leading individuals on their spiritual journey.

5. Practices and Techniques:


Thelema offers several practices to facilitate the Knowledge and Conversation of the Holy Guardian Angel, such as meditation, ceremonial rituals, and devotional practices. These methods focus on self-exploration, raising consciousness, and aligning with one’s divine purpose. The diligent pursuit of these practices within Thelema enables individuals to strengthen their connection to their HGA and gain profound insights into their spiritual path.


Aleister Crowley’s insights into guardian angels within the Thelema tradition provide a fascinating departure from conventional beliefs. The approach of Thelema towards the Holy Guardian Angel emphasizes the importance of self-discovery, aligning with one’s True Will, and fostering a deep and personal connection with the divine. By engaging in the practices and rituals prescribed in Thelema, individuals can embark on a transformative spiritual journey, unlocking the potential of their Higher Self and embracing their unique path.

item.title: The Divine Felines: Cats in Ancient Egypt, the Black Death, Witchcraft, and Aleister Crowley’s Fascination
item.dc:creator: Tobit
item.description: Cats have held a special place in human history, often associated with mysticism, magic, and divine powers. From their revered status in ancient Egypt to their role during the Black Death, as well as their connection to witchcraft and occultism, the captivating allure of these enigmatic creatures has transcended time. Furthermore, the opinions of influential figures such as Aleister Crowley have shed light on their significance and symbolism. 1. Cats: The Divine Beings of Ancient Egypt: In ancient Egypt, cats were regarded as sacred creatures and were revered as manifestations of various deities, notably Bastet, the goddess …
item.content:encoded:
Cats have held a special place in human history, often associated with mysticism, magic, and divine powers. From their revered status in ancient Egypt to their role during the Black Death, as well as their connection to witchcraft and occultism, the captivating allure of these enigmatic creatures has transcended time. Furthermore, the opinions of influential figures such as Aleister Crowley have shed light on their significance and symbolism.

1. Cats: The Divine Beings of Ancient Egypt:


In ancient Egypt, cats were regarded as sacred creatures and were revered as manifestations of various deities, notably Bastet, the goddess of protection, fertility, and home. They were highly regarded, often mummified, and buried with their owners to accompany them in the afterlife. Killing a cat, even accidentally, was considered sacrilegious and a grave offense.

2. Cats: The Unsung Heroes of the Black Death:


During the devastating period of the Black Death in Europe, cats played a crucial role in curbing the spread of the disease. As carriers of fleas, which were the primary vectors for the plague, cats instinctively hunted and eliminated rodents, particularly rats, which were responsible for harboring these fleas. Their valiant efforts inadvertently protected humans and helped save countless lives.

3. Cats and Witchcraft: A Feline Connection:


Unfortunately, the association between cats and witchcraft arose during the Middle Ages. Cats’ nocturnal habits, enigmatic behaviors, and their independent nature led them to be linked with witchcraft and dark practices. These associations led to cats being persecuted and killed during the infamous witch hunts, as they were seen as familiars or companions to witches.

4. Aleister Crowley’s Opinion on Cats:


Aleister Crowley, a renowned occultist and key figure in the esoteric tradition of the 20th century, had varied opinions on cats. While he did not explicitly express his views on cats in his writings, he did reference feline symbolism and imagery in his magical system known as Thelema. Crowley often associated cats with independence, sexuality, mystery, and the exploration of the deeper aspects of human consciousness.

5. Cats in Modern Occultism:


Cats continue to be intertwined with occultism and modern spiritual practices. Many practitioners of witchcraft, Wicca, and various pagan traditions perceive cats as spiritual allies and possessors of arcane knowledge. Their grace, stealth, and aloof yet intuitive nature make them potent symbols of esoteric exploration and the mysteries of the unconscious mind.
From their divine status in ancient Egypt to their instrumental role during the Black Death and subsequent association with witchcraft, cats have garnered an everlasting allure, representing the enigmatic but aloof character. Their mysterious nature has attracted the fascination of figures like Aleister Crowley, who saw in them a reflection of human sexuality and untamed spiritual power. As we continue to explore the realms of occultism, cats remain present, intertwined with symbolism and embodying the indomitable spirit of magic and mystery.

item.title: Aleister Crowley and the Influence of Occult Organizations on Thelema
item.dc:creator: Tobit
item.description: Aleister Crowley, a prominent figure in the occult world, was associated with several influential occult organizations during his lifetime. These associations played a significant role in shaping his spiritual and philosophical system known as Thelema. In this blog post, we will explore the similarities between Thelema and Freemasonry, the Hermetic Order of the Golden Dawn, as well as the Ordo Templi Orientis (O.T.O.) and the A∴A∴, shedding light on how Crowley drew inspiration from each of these organizations. Similarly, the A∴A∴, building on the spiritual path of the Golden Dawn, aimed to guide practitioners toward spiritual enlightenment …
item.content:encoded:
Aleister Crowley, a prominent figure in the occult world, was associated with several influential occult organizations during his lifetime. These associations played a significant role in shaping his spiritual and philosophical system known as Thelema. In this blog post, we will explore the similarities between Thelema and Freemasonry, the Hermetic Order of the Golden Dawn, as well as the Ordo Templi Orientis (O.T.O.) and the A∴A∴, shedding light on how Crowley drew inspiration from each of these organizations.

  1. Thelema and Freemasonry:
    Aleister Crowley was deeply influenced by Freemasonry, which he saw as a repository of ancient symbolism and initiatory practices. The concept of initiation, the use of ritual, and the hierarchical structure found in Freemasonry served as foundations for Thelema. However, Crowley believed that Freemasonry had lost touch with its original spiritual essence, prompting him to create a new system that he felt better aligned with his own beliefs.
  2. Thelema and the Hermetic Order of the Golden Dawn:
    Crowley’s involvement with the Hermetic Order of the Golden Dawn provided him with a wealth of knowledge in ceremonial magic, Qabalah, and the Western esoteric tradition. The Golden Dawn’s emphasis on tarot, astrology, and the use of ritual magic greatly influenced Thelema’s practices. The Golden Dawn’s emphasis on individual spiritual development and self-discovery also resonated profoundly with Crowley’s philosophy of personal freedom and self-realization.
  3. The O.T.O. and the A∴A∴:
    Crowley’s creation of the Ordo Templi Orientis (O.T.O.) and the Argentum Astrum (A∴A∴) demonstrated his desire to establish organizations that would serve as vehicles for the dissemination and exploration of Thelemic teachings. The O.T.O., inspired by Freemasonry and other mystical traditions, aimed to provide a framework for the promotion of Thelemic principles, including the pursuit of individual will and the exploration of sexual mysticism.

Similarly, the A∴A∴, building on the spiritual path of the Golden Dawn, aimed to guide practitioners toward spiritual enlightenment and self-realization through a system of grades and initiations. The A∴A∴ also embraced the concept of the True Will, emphasizing the importance of finding and aligning oneself with one’s true purpose in life.

Aleister Crowley’s involvement with various occult organizations played a substantial role in the development and design of Thelema. The prevalent influence of Freemasonry, the Hermetic Order of the Golden Dawn, the O.T.O., and the A∴A∴ is evident in Thelema’s symbolism, rituals, and spiritual concepts. While Crowley drew inspiration from these organizations, he also brought his unique perspective and ideas, resulting in the creation of a distinct spiritual system that continues to resonate with practitioners today. Understanding these influences allows us to appreciate the rich tapestry of ideas that shaped Thelema and its lasting impact on the occult world.
